message: Expand Microsoft OCSF mapping: PowerShell logging, audit log cleared, and IAM lifecycle (#143)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+---
+title: 'Expanded Windows Event Log OCSF mapping: PowerShell, audit log cleared, and
+  IAM lifecycle'
+type: feature
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+pr: 143
+created: 2026-03-24T13:37:04.933237Z
+---
+
+The `microsoft::windows::ocsf::map` operator now covers five additional Windows Event Log categories:
+
+**PowerShell logging** (EIDs 4100/4103/4104/4105/4106) maps to OCSF Script Activity (1009). EID 4104 (Script Block Logging) sets `severity_id` to Low when AMSI flags the block; EID 4100 (engine error) marks the execution as a failure.
+
+**Audit log cleared** (EID 1102) maps to OCSF Event Log Activity (1008, Clear) with `severity_id` set to High — clearing the security log is a strong attacker indicator (MITRE ATT&CK T1070.001).
+
+**Account Change** (EIDs 4720/4722–4726) replaces the previous create-only handler and now covers the full lifecycle: create, enable, disable, delete, password change, and password reset, using the correct OCSF 3001 activity IDs throughout.
+
+**Group Management** (EIDs 4727–4734/4754–4757) replaces the previous add-member-only handler and now covers global, local, and universal security group create, delete, add member, and remove member.

@@ -0,0 +1,44 @@
+---
+description: >
+  Account Change (EID 4720/4722–4726) → OCSF Account Change (3001)
+  4720 Create, 4722 Enable, 4723 Password Change, 4724 Password Reset,
+  4725 Disable, 4726 Delete
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.account_change"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 3
+ocsf.class_uid = 3001
+
+let $activities = {
+  "4720": 1,  // Create
+  "4722": 2,  // Enable
+  "4723": 3,  // Password Change
+  "4724": 4,  // Password Reset
+  "4725": 5,  // Disable
+  "4726": 6,  // Delete
+}
+ocsf.activity_id = $activities[ocsf.metadata.event_code]? else 99
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.actor = {
+  user: {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserSid,
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserName,
+    domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectDomainName,
+  },
+  session: {
+    uid_alt: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectLogonId,
+  },
+}
+
+ocsf.user = {
+  uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetSid,
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetUserName,
+  domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetDomainName,
+}
+
+// EID 4720 carries many account-attribute fields (DisplayName, HomeDirectory,
+// UserAccountControl flags, etc.) containing either real values or Windows
+// sentinel strings ("%%1793" = not set, "%%1794" = never).  All stay in
+// unmapped; downstream enrichment can decode %%NNNN message IDs if needed.

@@ -0,0 +1,69 @@
+---
+description: >
+  Windows Defender Attack Surface Reduction Block (EID 1121)
+  → OCSF Detection Finding (2004)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.detection_finding"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 2
+ocsf.class_uid = 2004
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Create
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+ocsf.metadata.profiles = ["host", "security_control"]
+
+// ASR blocks are always high-severity actionable events.
+ocsf.severity_id = 4  // High
+ocsf.disposition_id = 2  // Blocked
+
+ocsf.finding_info = {
+  title: ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Threat Name"],
+  uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Rule ID"],
+  desc: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Rule Name"],
+  types: ["ASR Rule Block"],
+}
+ocsf.malware = [{
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Threat Name"],
+}]
+
+// Process that was blocked.
+ocsf.actor = {
+  process: {
+    path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Process name"],
+    file: { type_id: 1 },
+  },
+}
+ocsf.actor.process.name = ocsf.actor.process.path.split("\\")[-1]
+ocsf.actor.process.file.name = ocsf.actor.process.name
+ocsf.actor.process.file.path = ocsf.actor.process.path
+
+// User is "DOMAIN\username" format.
+_user = (move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.User).split("\\")
+ocsf.actor.user = {
+  domain: _user[0],
+  name: _user[1],
+}
+drop _user
+
+// Affected file with hashes; wrap in evidences as required by Detection Finding.
+ocsf.file = {
+  path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Path,
+  type_id: 1,
+}
+ocsf.file.name = ocsf.file.path.split("\\")[-1]
+
+// Parse "SHA256=<hex>" or "MD5=<hex>,SHA256=<hex>,..." into hash objects.
+let $algorithms = {
+  MD5: 1,
+  SHA1: 2,
+  SHA256: 3,
+  SHA512: 4,
+}
+ocsf.file.hashes = ((move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Hashes)
+  .split(",")
+  .map(h => h.split("=", max=1))
+  .map(kv => {
+    algorithm_id: $algorithms[kv[0]?]? else 99,
+    value: kv[1]?,
+  }))
+ocsf.evidences = [{ file: move ocsf.file }]

@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+---
+description: >
+  Windows Defender Malware Detection/Remediation (EID 1116 Detected,
+  1117 Action Taken) → OCSF Detection Finding (2004)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.detection_finding"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 2
+ocsf.class_uid = 2004
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Create
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+ocsf.metadata.profiles = ["host", "security_control"]
+
+ocsf.severity_id = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Severity ID"]
+
+// Defender Action IDs → OCSF disposition_id (present on EID 1117).
+let $dispositions = {
+  "1": 11,  // Clean → Corrected
+  "2": 3,   // Quarantine → Quarantined
+  "3": 5,   // Remove → Deleted
+  "6": 1,   // Allow → Allowed
+  "9": 16,  // No Action → No Action
+  "10": 2,  // Block → Blocked
+}
+if ocsf.unmapped.EventData.has("Action ID") {
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[(move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Action ID"]).string()]? else 99
+}
+
+// Threat name is copied into finding_info.title then moved into malware.name.
+ocsf.finding_info = {
+  title: ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Threat Name"],
+  uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Detection ID"],
+  types: [move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Category Name"]],
+}
+ocsf.malware = [{
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Threat Name"],
+}]
+
+// Detected file; wrap in evidences as required by Detection Finding.
+ocsf.file = {
+  path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Path,
+  type_id: 1,  // Regular file
+}
+ocsf.file.name = ocsf.file.path.split("\\")[-1]
+ocsf.evidences = [{ file: move ocsf.file }]
+
+// Process that triggered the detection.
+ocsf.actor = {
+  process: {
+    path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Process Name"],
+    file: { type_id: 1 },
+  },
+}
+ocsf.actor.process.name = ocsf.actor.process.path.split("\\")[-1]
+ocsf.actor.process.file.name = ocsf.actor.process.name
+ocsf.actor.process.file.path = ocsf.actor.process.path
+
+// Detection User is "DOMAIN\username" format.
+_user = (move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Detection User"]).split("\\")
+ocsf.actor.user = {
+  domain: _user[0],
+  name: _user[1],
+}
+drop _user
+
+// Drop sentinel/redundant fields.
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Unused
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Unused2
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Unused3
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Severity Name"]
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Action Name"]
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Type Name"]
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Origin Name"]
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Execution Name"]
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Status Description"]
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Remediation User"]

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+---
+description: >
+  Windows Defender Signature Update (EID 2000)
+  → Base Event (0, 0)
+---
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 0
+ocsf.class_uid = 0
+ocsf.activity_id = 0

+// Signature version details stay in unmapped for downstream consumers.

+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Product Name"]?

@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+---
+description: >
+  Windows Defender Tamper Events (EID 5001 Real-Time Protection Disabled,
+  5007 Configuration Changed) → OCSF Detection Finding (2004)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.detection_finding"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 2
+ocsf.class_uid = 2004
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Create
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// AV tamper events are always high-severity.
+ocsf.severity_id = 4  // High
+
+let $titles = {
+  "5001": "Windows Defender Real-Time Protection Disabled",
+  "5007": "Windows Defender Configuration Changed",
+}
+ocsf.finding_info = {
+  title: $titles[ocsf.metadata.event_code]? else "Windows Defender Tamper",
+  uid: ocsf.metadata.original_event_uid,
+  types: ["Software: Antivirus"],
+}
+
+// "Product Name" is redundant with metadata.product.name.
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Product Name"]

@@ -0,0 +1,75 @@
+---
+description: >
+  Windows Defender Threat Found/Action (EID 1006 Found, 1007 Action)
+  → OCSF Detection Finding (2004)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.detection_finding"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 2
+ocsf.class_uid = 2004
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Create
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+ocsf.metadata.profiles = ["host", "security_control"]
+
+// Map string severity name to OCSF severity_id.
+let $severities = {
+  "Low": 2,
+  "Moderate": 3,
+  "High": 4,
+  "Severe": 5,
+}
+ocsf.severity_id = $severities[move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Severity]? else 0
+
+// Action string → OCSF disposition_id (EID 1007 only).
+let $dispositions = {
+  "Clean": 11,       // Corrected
+  "Quarantine": 3,   // Quarantined
+  "Remove": 5,       // Deleted
+  "Allow": 1,        // Allowed
+  "Block": 2,        // Blocked
+}
+if ocsf.unmapped.EventData.has("Action") {
+  ocsf.disposition_id = $dispositions[move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Action]? else 99
+}
+
+// Threat name is copied into finding_info.title then moved into malware.name.
+ocsf.finding_info = {
+  title: ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Threat Name"],
+  uid: (move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ID).string(),
+  types: [move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Category],
+}
+ocsf.malware = [{
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Threat Name"],
+}]
+
+// Detected file; wrap in evidences as required by Detection Finding (EID 1006 only).
+if ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Path? != null {
+  ocsf.file = {
+    path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Path,
+    type_id: 1,  // Regular file
+  }
+  ocsf.file.name = ocsf.file.path.split("\\")[-1]
+  ocsf.evidences = [{ file: move ocsf.file }]
+}
+
+// Process that triggered the detection (EID 1006 only).
+if ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Process Name"]? != null {
+  ocsf.actor = {
+    process: {
+      path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData["Process Name"],
+      file: { type_id: 1 },
+    },
+  }
+  ocsf.actor.process.name = ocsf.actor.process.path.split("\\")[-1]
+  ocsf.actor.process.file.name = ocsf.actor.process.name
+  ocsf.actor.process.file.path = ocsf.actor.process.path
+}
+
+// User is "DOMAIN\username" format.
+_user = (move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.User).split("\\")
+ocsf.actor.user = {
+  domain: _user[0],
+  name: _user[1],
+}
+drop _user

@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+---
+description: Audit Log Cleared (EID 1102) → OCSF Event Log Activity (1008, Clear)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.event_log_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 1008
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Clear
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// Clearing the security audit log is a high-severity indicator of an attacker
+// covering their tracks (MITRE ATT&CK T1070.001).
+ocsf.severity_id = 4  // High
+
+ocsf.actor = {
+  user: {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserSid,
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserName,
+    domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectDomainName,
+  },
+  session: {
+    uid_alt: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectLogonId,
+  },
+  process: {
+    pid: int(move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ClientProcessId, base=16),
+  },
+}
+
+// metadata.log_name already carries the channel (Security) from map.tql.
+// ClientProcessStartKey has no OCSF home; left in unmapped.

@@ -0,0 +1,54 @@
+---
+description: >
+  Security Group Lifecycle (EID 4727–4730 global, 4731–4734 local,
+  4754–4758 universal) → OCSF Group Management (3006)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.group_management"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 3
+ocsf.class_uid = 3006
+
+let $activities = {
+  // Create group
+  "4727": 6, "4731": 6, "4754": 6,
+  // Delete group
+  "4730": 5, "4734": 5, "4758": 5,
+  // Add member
+  "4728": 3, "4732": 3, "4756": 3,
+  // Remove member
+  "4729": 4, "4733": 4, "4757": 4,
+  // Group changed (universal group modified — no closer activity)
+  "4755": 99,
+}
+ocsf.activity_id = $activities[ocsf.metadata.event_code]? else 99
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.actor = {
+  user: {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserSid,
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserName,
+    domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectDomainName,
+  },
+  session: {
+    uid_alt: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectLogonId,
+  },
+}
+
+// TargetUser* fields describe the group being acted on (not a user account).
+ocsf.group = {
+  uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetSid,
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetUserName,
+  domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetDomainName,
+}
+
+// Member fields only present on add/remove events (4728/4729/4732/4733/4756/4757).
+if ocsf.unmapped.EventData.MemberSid? != null {
+  ocsf.user = {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.MemberSid,
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.MemberName,
+  }
+}
+
+// PrivilegeList is always "-" on these events; drop to keep unmapped clean.
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData.PrivilegeList

@@ -0,0 +1,41 @@
+---
+description: PowerShell Engine Error (EID 4100) → OCSF Script Activity (1009, Execute/Failure)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.script_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 1009
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Execute
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.status_id = 2  // Failure — this event fires on PS engine errors
+ocsf.severity_id = 2  // Low — Windows Level 3 (Warning)
+ocsf.status_code = (move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ErrorCode).string()
+ocsf.status_detail = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Message
+
+// HostApplication is the full command line (exe + args), not just the path.
+ocsf.actor = {
+  process: {
+    cmd_line: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.HostApplication,
+    file: { type_id: 8 },
+  },
+}
+
+// User is in "DOMAIN\username" format.
+_user = (move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.User).split("\\")
+ocsf.actor.user = {
+  domain: _user[0],
+  name: _user[1],
+}
+drop _user
+
+ocsf.script = {
+  type_id: 2,  // PowerShell
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.CommandInvocation,
+}
+
+// CommandLine (the full command attempted), HostVersion, EngineVersion,
+// RunspaceId, PipelineId, ScriptName, ShellId — left in unmapped.
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData.HostName
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ConnectedUser

@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+---
+description: PowerShell Module / Pipeline Logging (EID 4103) → OCSF Script Activity (1009, Execute)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.script_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 1009
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Execute
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// Windows Level 4 (Information).
+ocsf.severity_id = 1  // Informational
+
+ocsf.script = {
+  type_id: 2,  // PowerShell
+}
+
+// Payload contains the command invocation and parameter bindings as plain text;
+// preserve verbatim in unmapped — parsing it would require regex surgery and
+// the raw content is already in raw_data.
+//
+// ContextInfo is a multi-line key=value block with host, engine, runspace,
+// pipeline, user, and shell info; also left in unmapped for the same reason.

@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+---
+description: PowerShell Script Block Logging (EID 4104) → OCSF Script Activity (1009, Execute)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.script_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 1009
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Execute
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// Windows Level 3 (Warning) when AMSI flags the block; Level 5 (Verbose) for
+// benign blocks.  Map Level 3 → Low (2), everything else → Informational (1).
+ocsf.severity_id = 2 if ocsf.metadata.log_level == "3" else 1
+
+ocsf.script = {
+  uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ScriptBlockId,
+  type_id: 2,  // PowerShell
+}
+
+// Path is the script file path when the block originates from a file;
+// empty string means an interactive/in-memory block.
+if ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Path? != null and ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Path? != "" {
+  ocsf.script.file = {
+    path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Path,
+    type_id: 1,  // Regular file
+  }
+  ocsf.script.file.name = ocsf.script.file.path.split("\\")[-1]
+  ocsf.script.name = ocsf.script.file.name
+}
+
+// ScriptBlockText is the raw script content — potentially large and already
+// preserved verbatim in raw_data. MessageNumber/MessageTotal indicate chunked
+// multi-part blocks. Both stay in unmapped for downstream consumers.

@@ -0,0 +1,28 @@
+---
+description: >
+  PowerShell Script Block Invocation Start/Stop (EID 4105/4106)
+  → OCSF Script Activity (1009, Execute)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.script_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 1009
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Execute
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// Windows Level 5 (Verbose).
+ocsf.severity_id = 1  // Informational
+
+// These events carry only correlation IDs; their value is linking back to the
+// matching 4104 script-block record via script.uid / actor.session.uid.
+ocsf.script = {
+  uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ScriptBlockId,
+  type_id: 2,  // PowerShell
+}
+
+ocsf.actor = {
+  session: {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.RunspaceId,
+  },
+}

+---
+description: >
+  Task Scheduler Lifecycle (EID 106 Register, 140 Update, 141 Delete)
+  → OCSF Scheduled Job Activity (1006)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.scheduled_job_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 1006
+
+let $activities = {
+  "106": 1,  // Create
+  "140": 2,  // Update
+  "141": 3,  // Delete
+}
+ocsf.activity_id = $activities[ocsf.metadata.event_code]? else 99
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.job = {
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TaskName,
+}
+
+// UserContext is "DOMAIN\username" format.
+_user = (move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.UserContext).split("\\")
+ocsf.actor = {
+  user: {
+    domain: _user[0],
+    name: _user[1],
+  },
+}
+drop _user
